title: AI / ML Research
description: Research-oriented path into machine learning and AI. Heavy math, best runway to grad school, research labs, and cutting-edge industry roles.
why_this_hint: If you're drawn to how machines learn from data — or you want to go to grad school — this path starts with low-barrier clubs and builds toward paid summer research.

title: Cybersecurity
description: Hands-on path in offensive and defensive security. CTF competitions, systems knowledge, and a strong pipeline to security internships and industry roles.
why_this_hint: If breaking and defending systems sounds more exciting than building them, start with ACM Cyber's weekly CTF sessions — no prior experience needed.
sample_downstream: Play first CTF — picoCTF warmup track

title: Full-Stack + Industry
description: Build-first path focused on shipping products and landing software engineering internships. Lower research ceiling, faster job market entry.
why_this_hint: If you want to ship things people actually use — and get a SWE internship by junior year — this path runs from club projects straight into recruiting season.
sample_downstream: Join ACM Hack — ship an app in a quarter
Prep SWE recruiting at the UCLA Career Center

title: I'm not sure yet — help me explore
description: Not sure which direction to go? Walk three futures in parallel: AI/ML research, cybersecurity, and full-stack product — see which one pulls you in.
why_this_hint: The best next move when you're undecided isn't picking one path — it's taking one low-cost step on each so the right one becomes obvious.
sample_downstream: Email an AI lab PI for a 15-min tour
Attend ACM AI or ACM Cyber first meeting

title: Policy Journalism
description: Long-form research and reporting about civic life, government, and institutions. Strong pipeline into think tanks, legal fellowships, and investigative newsrooms.
why_this_hint: If your writing already reads like civic research — think URFP papers, Daily Bruin features — formalize it with a Public Affairs or Digital Humanities minor and a Bruin news-desk pitch.
sample_downstream: Summer Dow Jones News Fund fellowship (apply fall)

title: Product & UX Writing
description: Narrative design for digital products — microcopy, content strategy, onboarding flows. High demand, low competition from humanities grads.
why_this_hint: Editorial judgment + a little Figma is already 80% of a UX writing portfolio. Start with Creative Labs or Bruin Consulting; ship two case studies.
sample_downstream: Ship 2 UX writing case studies on personal site

title: Humanities Research → Grad School
description: Academic path: seminars, URFP, conference papers, faculty relationships, PhD pipeline. Slow burn, high ceiling.
why_this_hint: If you love archives and long arguments, URFP + a close advisor relationship is the unlock. Start by emailing a professor whose work you already quote.
sample_downstream: Email a faculty advisor for office hours
Target one UCLA research symposium submission

title: Data for Public Policy
description: Quantitative methods applied to social problems — housing, health, education. Bridges social science with data skills. Strong fit for Urban Planning / Public Policy grad school.
why_this_hint: A social science background + Python/R + one public-sector project is rarer than you'd think. UCLA Luskin and DataRes both have undergrad tracks.

title: Bridge a Pivot
description: For students mid-pivot: stay in current major, build a portable skill bridge (data, design, writing, research) that makes the next move cheap.
why_this_hint: Pivots don't require switching majors. Build two portable artifacts this year that work in either direction — then decide with data.
sample_downstream: Pick 1 skill to bridge (data / design / writing)
Office hours with advisor to map credit overlap
